title: Allow configuring Twitch OAuth callback port
description: Right now the Twitch OAuth callback server listens on a single hardcoded port in the 37000-37100 range. Add a settings control (Extra Settings API) so hosts can override the port if their machine conflicts with the default. The control should default to the current hardcoded port value, persist via the Extra Settings API (likely using the auth datastore or a dedicated setting), and the authorization flow should read that value whenever it starts the local listener. Include validation (e.g., restrict to 1024-65535) and meaningful error messaging if the port is invalid or already bound. Update README/CHANGELOG to explain the new option and any firewall considerations.

title: Update Twitch authentication to use new OAuth refresh tokens
description: Twitch changed their authentication requirements and the legacy `twitchToken` no longer works for connecting the mod's IRC client. Update the mod to use the new settings fields defined in `modinfo.json` (`twitchClientId`, `twitchRefreshToken`, `twitchAccessToken`) and persist them via the Extra Settings API data store. Implement token refresh logic using Twitch's OAuth refresh endpoint so the mod can automatically recover from expired access tokens. Update documentation to explain the new setup.
acceptance_criteria:
- The mod no longer relies on the legacy `twitchToken` for authentication
- Access, refresh, and client ID values are persisted via Extra Settings API data storage rather than plain input fields
- Token refresh flow implemented using Twitch OAuth refresh endpoint (https://dev.twitch.tv/docs/authentication/refresh-tokens/)
- When authentication fails due to expired tokens, the mod refreshes them and reconnects automatically, persisting new tokens
- README or changelog updated to document the new authentication requirements

title: Implement native Twitch OAuth authorization flow
description: We started replacing the manual paste-in of Twitch access/refresh tokens and client IDs with a proper OAuth authorization button in the mod settings. The new settings button already exists, but the flow is unfinished: clicking it should open the Twitch authorization URL in the player’s default browser (so password managers and security prompts work), and the mod must receive the callback to capture access and refresh tokens as well as the client ID. Finish wiring up the button so it launches the native browser, handles the redirect locally in the mod, and persists the resulting credentials via the Extra Settings API. Include any necessary documentation updates so players understand the new authorization process.
acceptance_criteria:
- Pressing the new Twitch authorization button opens the Twitch OAuth consent page in the user’s default system browser
- The mod spins up whatever callback handler is needed to receive the authorization code securely and exchange it for access/refresh tokens (and client ID if provided)
- Retrieved tokens/ID are stored via the Extra Settings API in the same fields used by the rest of the mod
- Error handling and user-facing messaging added for auth failures or cancellations
- README/CHANGELOG updated to describe the new OAuth button flow and removal of manual token copy/paste
notes: Hit socket binding error while testing the callback listener ("An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions."). Need to allow dynamic port selection or expose retryable failure path.

title: Other players cannot see the chosen shark name in multiplayer
description:
A Discord user reported that while the mod works on the host, other people on the server never see the chosen shark name. No notification reaches them and the in-game help does not explain how to propagate the selected name to the rest of the session. We need to reproduce the issue in a multiplayer session, verify how shark names are synchronized, and ensure the correct name (and notification) is shown to all players.

Bug report summary: "pool there was no notif. We will have to do more testing. The issue is that the mod does work, but no one else sees the correct name. The help doesn't really help me figure out how to make other people on the server see the chosen name."

Scope:
- Reproduce the bug in multiplayer and capture logs.
- Investigate why the selected shark name fails to sync to other participants.
- Fix synchronization so every client sees the correct name and receives any related notifications.
- Update the user-facing help text (if needed) explaining multiplayer behavior.

Deliverables:
- Code fix (or configuration change) that ensures shark names are visible to all players in the session.
- Tests or manual verification notes covering the multiplayer scenario.
- Updated documentation/help text to clarify multiplayer requirements if relevant.
acceptance_criteria:
- Multiplayer session reproduction steps documented and verified
- When a shark name is selected, all connected players see the same name and notification
- Fix validated via manual test or automated coverage describing the multiplayer scenario
- Updated troubleshooting/help text explaining any necessary multiplayer configuration (if relevant)
- Root cause and fix summarized in the changelog or equivalent release note

title: Implement dynamic Twitch connection UI and disconnect handling
description:
Players now authenticate via the built-in Twitch OAuth button, and we want the settings UI to reflect the current connection state using the Extra Settings API. Implement the quality-of-life behavior discussed: when the mod is disconnected (no saved token/username), show the authorization button and hide the username field. Once connected, hide the authorization button, show the authenticated Twitch username as read-only text, and provide a "Disconnect" button. Clicking disconnect should clear stored credentials and update the UI.

Extra Settings API already has `ExtraSettingsAPI_SetSettingText` wired for updating the username display. Review the API wiki/source to discover how to toggle setting visibility/enabled state (e.g., `SetSettingVisible`, etc.) and add any missing interop wrappers if needed. Ensure the new button is handled via `ExtraSettingsAPI_ButtonPress`. Update the README/CHANGELOG if user guidance changes.

Include error handling/logs so we can diagnose failures to toggle UI elements, and ensure the button state persists correctly across reloads by checking stored auth state during initialization.
acceptance_criteria:
- When no access token/username is stored, the settings UI shows the "Authorize" button and hides the username text/Disconnect button
- After successful OAuth, the UI hides "Authorize", shows the username text populated from `/oauth2/validate`, and reveals the "Disconnect" button
- Clicking "Disconnect" clears stored credentials, reverts the UI to the disconnected state, and stops any Twitch session
- Extra Settings API visibility helpers are implemented per the upstream wiki/source so we interact with the API correctly
- Documentation (README/CHANGELOG) updated if instructions to players change
